brief_summary: Three cards on a brighter-to-deeper sound spectrum, plotting the same Oil King switches in three different chassis. Left (warm-grey outline): Bakeneko65 (unibody integrated, slightly metallic). Middle: Geon Frog (stiffer top-mount, more articulate). Right (bordeaux-filled, focal): Mode Sonnet (leaf-gasket soft mount, deeper, half-octave lower). Spectrum dots scale with how far each lands toward the deeper end.
data_sources: building-mode-sonnet-with-oil-kings.mdx § 'Sound check, post-build' — 'Compared to the same switches in a Bakeneko65 built last summer, the Sonnet sounds about a half-octave lower and considerably less metallic. Compared to a Geon Frog with Oil Kings ... the Sonnet is softer in feel and slightly muddier in sound. The Frog is more articulate; the Sonnet is more comfortable.'
